question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user submit repeated GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto requests with the maximum allowed spender.id repeated query parameters to exhaust the database connection pool, causing all subsequent transaction queries to stall for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user craft a request with the maximum number of spender.id eq-operator values to force updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to build an unbounded SQL IN() clause, causing a full-table scan that delays block-time-equivalent query processing by 500%+?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user supply a valid but computationally expensive EVM address as idOrAliasOrEvmAddress to EntityService.getEncodedId(), triggering a slow entity resolution path that cumulatively delays the node's response pipeline beyond 500% of average block time under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user flood the /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto endpoint with concurrent requests using a high limit= value to saturate the database read I/O, effectively freezing transaction-related queries for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user submit requests with the maximum allowed repeated spender.id gt/lt/gte/lte operators to force updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues() to append a large number of individual SQL conditions, causing query plan degradation that delays responses by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the allowances/crypto endpoint to issue thousands of concurrent requests, exhausting Node.js event loop capacity and causing a processing freeze exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a slow database query by requesting allowances for an account with an extremely large number of spender relationships, causing CryptoAllowanceService.getAccountCryptoAllowances() to hold a DB connection for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user send a request with limit= set to the maximum allowed value combined with order=asc and multiple spender.id filters to force a worst-case sequential scan in CryptoAllowanceService, delaying block processing by 500%+?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user abuse the pagination link returned in response.links.next to chain an unbounded sequence of paginated requests, each consuming a DB connection, cumulatively freezing the node's query throughput for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user supply a non-existent but syntactically valid account alias as idOrAliasOrEvmAddress to force EntityService.getEncodedId() into a slow lookup path, and repeat this at scale to delay block-time-equivalent processing by 500%+?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user send a request with the maximum config.query.maxRepeatedQueryParameters spender.id eq values to cause updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to build a SQL IN() clause with hundreds of positional parameters ($1..$N), causing query compilation overhead that delays responses by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of per-IP request throttling on the allowances/crypto endpoint to sustain a high request rate that keeps the PostgreSQL connection pool fully occupied, blocking all other transaction-related queries for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user send requests with a crafted EVM address that triggers a full entity table scan in EntityService.getEncodedId(), and repeat this concurrently to delay the mirror node's block ingestion pipeline by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user mix eq and range (gt/lt) spender.id operators in a single request to maximize the number of SQL conditions appended to the conditions array, causing query plan instability that delays responses by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the async nature of getAccountCryptoAllowances() by sending a burst of requests that all await CryptoAllowanceService.getAccountCryptoAllowances() simultaneously, exhausting the async I/O queue and freezing block-time-equivalent processing for 500%+?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged user send a request with limit= at the maximum allowed value and order=desc to force CryptoAllowanceService to sort and return the maximum result set, consuming enough DB CPU to delay block-time-equivalent processing by 500%+?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the CryptoAllowanceViewModel mapping step (allowances.map((allowance) => new CryptoAllowanceViewModel(allowance))) by requesting maximum-limit results for an account with many allowances, causing memory pressure that delays block processing by 500%+?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/cryptoAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that getAccountCryptoAllowances() performs no request deduplication to send identical concurrent requests for the same account, causing the DB to execute the same expensive query multiple times simultaneously and delaying block processing by 500%+?
